blocks.heading: Prologue Part A: November 29
blocks.content:
Start a new game and select the game's difficulty level. If you plan on getting the Platinum trophy, you will need at least two playthroughs. If you have save data from *Trails of Cold Steel*, you can transfer your save file over for extra bonuses (see tables below).

<!-- checkpoint: pro-new-game | Started New Game -->

blocks.heading: Difficulty Settings
rows: Balanced for quick, easy battles. Ideal for beginners and story enthusiasts.
Enemies are at 0.70 of their stats.
Build around satisfyingly tough combat. Aimed at people used to playing RPGs.
Enemies are at 1.00 of their stats.
Battles will take everything you have. For skilled players who find Normal too easy.
Enemies are at 1.15 of their stats.
Balanced for New Game+. Ideal for replays.
Enemies are at 1.30 of their stats.

blocks.heading: Transfer Bonuses: Rean's Level
rows: Sepith x100 (All Colors), Attack 1, Zeram Powder
Sepith x300 (All Colors), Attack 1, Soul Blur, Zeram Powder
Sepith x500 (All Colors), Attack 1, Soul Blur, Saintly Force, Zeram Powder

blocks.content: Watch the introduction sequence carefully. If you haven't beaten the original *Trails of Cold Steel*, this part will give you a summary of the ending — **beware of spoilers**.

blocks.heading: Taking Control & Basic Mechanics
blocks.content:
After the intro sequence ends, you'll take control of **Rean**. Enemy information in this guide is based on **Normal mode** circumstances.

The game immediately introduces the **save system** — open the menu and use the **Save** option at any time. You can also use the **Cross-Save feature** to upload save data to PSN for transferring between PS3 and Vita.

If you ever get lost, press the **Square** button to open the **Navigation menu**, which highlights your next objective on the bottom right of the screen.

blocks.heading: Eisengard Range — First Steps
blocks.content:
Head downhill from your starting position. You'll spot a shining object — examine it to receive Rean's equipment. The game then introduces the **Orbment** system; open the menu and equip your Master Quartz under the Orbment section.

Continue running down the hill to encounter your first monster. Touch it to engage in battle.

> **Tip:** Sneak up on monsters or stun them with your field attack (press **X** / **O** in Japanese version) for an **advantage** in battle — extra turns and reduced enemy HP.

After your first victory, the game introduces **Monster Notes**. Each defeated monster fills in additional data in the bestiary. To complete entries instantly, use a **Battle Scope** item, the **Analyze** quartz spell, or **Emma and Eliot's Detector ability**.

<!-- checkpoint: pro-first-battle | First Battle Complete -->

blocks.heading: Eisengard Range — Arts & Healing
blocks.content:
Continue east to trigger an event scene, then fight a rock-type creature. The game teaches you about **Arts** — this monster has high physical defense, so use the **Fire Bolt** spell to take it down quickly.

Further down the hill, Rean notices an **Orbment Charging Station**. Examine it to fully restore HP and EP. When ready, proceed to the next zone: **Eisengard Mountains 2**.

blocks.heading: Eisengard Range — S-Crafts & Exploration
blocks.content:
Head down the hill to find a treasure chest containing a **Tear** quartz. A short distance ahead, Rean spots a particularly strong monster. Engage it — this fight introduces the **S-Craft** system. S-Crafts are super attacks requiring 100–200 CP (found under the Craft section). You can also trigger them by pressing **Triangle** outside your turn, though this adds significant delay to your next action.

Explore the area thoroughly:
- **South corner:** Treasure chest with a **Teara Balm**
- **East area:** Event scene
- **Northeast corner:** Treasure box containing a **Proxy Puppet** accessory

Take the southeast exit to reach **Eisengard Mountain 3**. Immediately head south for a treasure box with a **Celestial Balm**, then find the **Orbment Charging Station** at the south end to heal up. When ready, proceed to the southeast end for a boss battle.

<!-- checkpoint: pro-eisengard-explored | Eisengard Range Explored -->
Maps in this guide are designed with the compass facing north.

blocks.name: Magic Knight Ortheim
blocks.heading: Boss: Magic Knight Ortheim
blocks.strategy:
The first boss is straightforward. Ortheim attacks with its sword.

**Attack:** Use **Flame Strike** to deal damage — it has a chance to inflict **Burn**, which helps expedite the fight.

**Healing:** Heal when Rean drops below 3000 HP with **Tear** or a **Teara Balm**. If the boss gets 2 consecutive turns, use a **Teara Balm** immediately.

**Victory:** Once you bring it under 15,000 HP, the battle ends automatically.

blocks.heading: Prologue Part B: Arriving in Ymir
blocks.content:
Watch the event scene — you'll be reunited with **Elise**, **Alfin**, and **Toval**. After the scene, leave the building. The game introduces the **event system**: **story events** are marked with a **red exclamation mark**, while **side events** use a **green exclamation mark**.

<!-- checkpoint: pro-ymir-arrival | Arrived in Ymir -->

blocks.heading: Ymir — Shopping & Exploration
blocks.content:
Head to the **General Store "Plover"** in the southwest corner of town. Purchase **Imperial Chronicle Issue #1** and **Gambler Jack Reprint Chapter 1** — there's a trophy for collecting all books in the game, so don't skip these.

<!-- missable: pro-book-chronicle-1 | Imperial Chronicle Issue #1 -->
<!-- missable: pro-book-gambler-1 | Gambler Jack Reprint Chapter 1 -->

blocks.heading: Ymir — Story Events
blocks.content:
Visit all the red exclamation mark locations around town:

**Rean's Parents** — Find **Lucia** and **Baron Schwarzer** at the **Baron's Mansion**.

**Elise and Celine** — They're at the **Footbath** in the center of town. Speaking to them adds the "Friends Note" to your menu; collecting all profile pages earns a bronze trophy.

**Toval** — He's at the **Inn & Pub "Valley Echo"**. While there, speak to pub owner **Gerald** for pancakes.

**Werner** — Find him in the kitchen area of **The Phoenix Wings** hotel (northeast on map). Speaking to him adds the recipe section to your menu and unlocks a new recipe.

**Princess Alfin** — She's at the **Septian Church**.

**Annabelle** — Located at **Ymir Canyon** entrance. She gives you a fishing rod (adds the fishing section to your menu). If you transferred a save from the original *Cold Steel*, she provides **Groundbait** based on your rank. You can also exchange prizes with her.

<!-- checkpoint: pro-ymir-events | Completed Ymir Events -->

blocks.heading: Ymir — Advancing the Story
blocks.content:
Once you've completed all activities, head to **The Phoenix Wings** hotel and speak to the receptionist, **Baggins**. Then go to the **Hot Springs** on the right side of the building.

> **Warning:** Entering the Hot Spring automatically advances the story. Finish all shopping and events before going in!

After the hot spring scene, **Elise** and **Toval** join your party. Go back inside the **Baron Mansion** and speak to **Alfin** for a short event. Before heading out, use the **footbath** at the center of town to fully restore your party's HP, EP, and CP. Then take the **northwest exit** to reach **Ymir Canyon**.

<!-- checkpoint: pro-ymir-departure | Left Ymir for Canyon -->

blocks.heading: Nightmare Mode Preparation
blocks.content:
If playing on Nightmare difficulty (or preparing for a tough run), stock up before entering the canyon:

- **Healing:** Aim for 15–20 **Teara Balms** and **Reviving Balms**
- **Farming:** Cook **Salty Onigiri** with Elise to create **Tri-Colored Onigiri** (superb result) — sell for 150 Mira each to fund supplies
- **Equipment:** Ensure all three characters have the best accessory from the store (boosts Strength, ATS, and Speed)
- **Consumables:** Invest remaining funds in **EP Charges**

blocks.heading: Ymir Canyon — Link Attacks
blocks.content: Watch the event scene upon entering the canyon. When you encounter the first monster, the game introduces **Link Attacks** — hitting enemies with the right attack type can cause them to become unbalanced, triggering a follow-up **Link Attack** for bonus damage. Finish off the first enemy and continue deeper into the canyon.

blocks.heading: Ymir Canyon — Exploration & Treasures
blocks.content:
Explore the first area thoroughly:

**Northeast end:** Open the treasure chest for the **Lighter** accessory.

**Northwest path:** Follow it to find a monster chest containing **Zeram Powder**. This fight has eight enemies — use **Toval's S-Craft** to kill all eight at once for the **Pieces of Eight** trophy.

<!-- missable: pro-pieces-of-eight | Pieces of Eight Trophy -->

Backtrack south and cross the bridge heading east. Pick up the **U-Material x3** from the chest at the east end. Then take the **north exit** to reach **Ymir Canyon 2**.

<!-- checkpoint: pro-ymir-canyon-1 | Ymir Canyon 1 Complete -->

blocks.heading: Ymir Canyon 2 — To the Boss
blocks.content:
Grab the **Tearal Balm** from the chest as you head north. Take the northwest path where it splits:

- **South fork:** Treasure box with a **Poison Quartz**
- **North fork:** Follow it all the way to find a chest with **Sepith (All Colors x50)**, then continue just a bit further to reach an **Orbment Charging Station** — heal up completely before proceeding to the next area for the boss fight.

<!-- checkpoint: pro-ymir-canyon-2 | Ymir Canyon 2 Complete -->

blocks.name: Magic Knight Ortheim (Ymir Canyon)
blocks.heading: Boss: Magic Knight Ortheim (Ymir Canyon)
blocks.strategy:
Ortheim is significantly tougher this time around.

**Boss Skills:**
- **Sword Slash:** Hits everyone in front
- **Ground Stab:** Targets a large area around the boss
- **Boost:** Grants **STR 50% Up** and restores **15,000 HP**

**Strategy:**
- **Buff management:** When Ortheim uses Boost, it gains 50% attack power AND heals 20% of its HP — this prolongs the fight significantly. Push damage hard before it buffs.
- **Healing:** Use **Elise** as your dedicated healer with **Holy Squall** (restores 60% HP in a small radius) or **Tear** for single-target recovery.
- **DPS:** **Rean** and **Toval** are your main damage dealers. Rely on physical attacks and save your **S-Crafts** for turns with a critical bonus for maximum impact.

blocks.name: Magic Knight Ortheim (Phase 2) — Ashen Knight, Valimar
blocks.heading: Boss: Magic Knight Ortheim (Phase 2) — Ashen Knight, Valimar
blocks.strategy:
This is your introduction to **Divine Knight (mech) combat**, which works like rock-paper-scissors — you select a body part to attack and try to find the weak point.

**Stances & Weak Points:**
- **Idle Stance:** Attack the **Head** (★★★★ effectiveness)
- **Attack Stance:** Attack the **Arms** (★★★★ effectiveness)

Landing a hit on the correct body part scores a **critical hit**, allowing you to perform a **Link Attack** for bonus damage. Follow the stance pattern above and this fight is straightforward.

<!-- checkpoint: pro-valimar-victory | Defeated Ortheim with Valimar -->
